patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH v3 2/3] btrfs: fix incorrect buffered IO fallback for append direct writes

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v7 1/3] tracing/lock: Remove unnecessary linux/sched.h include

version_history.subject: [PATCH v7 1/3] tracing/lock: Remove unnecessary linux/sched.h include

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch removes an unnecessary include statement in the lock tracing header file, as none of the trace events reference anything from linux/sched.h.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v7 3/3] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to sleepable locks

version_history.subject: [PATCH v7 3/3] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to sleepable locks

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release to the Linux kernel's locking mechanism. It fires when a lock is released under contention, allowing for correlation of lock hold time with waiter events by lock address. The patch modifies the slowpath unlock paths of various sleepable locks, including mutexes, rtmutexes, semaphores, and rwsems, to enable this tracepoint. The placement of the tracepoint varies depending on the lock type, but it generally fires before the lock is released or after the release but before the wake. This patch also adds support for reader/writer locks, firing the tracepoint for every reader releasing while a writer is waiting.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v7 2/3] locking/percpu-rwsem: Extract __percpu_up_read()
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch extracts the slowpath of percpu_up_read() into a new function __percpu_up_read(). The original function is simplified, and the slowpath is moved to a separate function to avoid increasing binary size due to the addition of a tracepoint. The new function is exported as a symbol, allowing it to be used elsewhere in the kernel.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v7 0/3] locking: contended_release tracepoint instrumentation
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new tracepoint, contended_release, which fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This provides hold time estimation and allows for capturing the holder's stack at release time.

review_comments.author: Usama Arif
review_comments.summary: Acked the patch series without any objections or suggestions.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Thu,  4 Jun 2026 07:15:07 +0000 Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

> Add the contended_release trace event. This tracepoint fires on the
> holder side when a contended lock is released, complementing the
> existing contention_begin/contention_end tracepoints which fire on the
> waiter side.
> 
> This enables correlating lock hold time under contention with waiter
> events by lock address.
> 
> Add trace_contended_release()/trace_call__contended_release() calls to
> the slowpath unlock paths of sleepable locks: mutex, rtmutex, semaphore,
> rwsem, percpu-rwsem, and RT-specific rwbase locks.
> 
> Where possible, trace_contended_release() fires before the lock is
> released and before the waiter is woken. For some lock types, the
> tracepoint fires after the release but before the wake. Making the
> placement consistent across all lock types is not worth the added
> complexity.
> 
> For reader/writer locks, the tracepoint fires for every reader releasing
> while a writer is waiting, not only for the last reader.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>
> Acked-by: Paul E. McKenney <paulmck@kernel.org>

Acked-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
